channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas to free 3 more Israeli hostages for dozens of Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Hamas-led militants are set to free three more hostages, all Israeli civilian men, and Israel will release dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of a fragile agreement that has paused the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Trump says 'we will have relations with North Korea'; it's a 'big asset' that he gets along with Kim
item.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba at the White House and touted his good relationship with Kim Jong Un from meetings in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department lays out plans for $7 billion-plus arms sale to Israel as Netanyahu visits DC
item.description: The State Department has told Congress that it plans to sell more than $7 billion in weapons to Israel, including thousands of bombs and missiles, just two days after President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazil's socialist president tells citizens not to buy expensive grocery items as food prices rise
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a video message posted to social media that citizens should not buy expensive food items to deal with rising food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at the hostages still held by Hamas in Gaza, by the numbers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas are in the midst of a six-week ceasefire during which Hamas is releasing dozens of the hostages captured in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine's Western backers will meet for arms talks as doubts over US intentions grow
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The main international forum for drumming up weapons and ammunition for Ukraine will for the first time meet under the auspices of a country other than the U.S. as uncertainty surrounds the future of Washington’s support for arming the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The burnt-out shell of London's Grenfell Tower will be demolished, almost 8 years after deadly fire
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. government on Friday announced plans to dismantle the remains of London’s Grenfell Tower, almost eight years after the deadliest fire in Britain since World War II swept through the high-rise apartment building, killing 72 people.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Panama's president denies making a deal that US warships can transit the canal for free
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino on Thursday denied the U.S. State Department’s claim that his country had reached a deal allowing U.S. warships to transit the Panama Canal for free. Mulino said he had told U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unions sue Trump administration over USAID shutdown
item.description: Unions representing government employees sued the Trump administration Thursday evening to block efforts to shut down the government’s independent foreign assistance agency. Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) launched a full-scale assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration slashing USAID workforce to fewer than 300 employees: Reports
item.description: The government's foremost foreign aid agency is being gutted. The U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) workforce is being slashed from more than 10,000 employees to fewer than 300, according to reports published in Wired, Reuters, The New York Times and NPR.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Future looks dire for UN Palestinian refugee agency, says UNRWA chief
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The head of the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Thursday that while an Israeli ban has not yet forced the agency to cease operations, it faces an “existential threat” in the long run.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US targets network accused of facilitating Iranian oil shipments to China with sanctions
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury on Thursday announced new sanctions on an international network of individuals and companies accused of facilitating Iranian oil shipments to China.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions and concern about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: US ally cautions world against doubting Trump's 'shockingly innovative' Gaza proposal
item.description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Szijjártó admits that President Donald Trump's proposals can be "shockingly innovative," but says that the world should not dismiss his Gaza Strip idea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House flags top USAID boondoggles under Elon Musk's microscope
item.description: The White House has released a list of USAID programs its considers wasteful and that it says abused taxpayer funds by backing programs that supported DEI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation
